---
editor_options: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

------------------------------------------------------------------------

title: "ParkSmart" subtitle: "Documentación de Base de Datos — MySQL y
PostgreSQL" author: "Desarrollo Web — 2026" date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document: toc: true toc_float: true theme: flatly
highlight: tango number_sections: true ---------------------

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Introducción

El presente documento describe la implementación de la base de datos
**ParkSmart**, un sistema orientado a la gestión de parqueaderos,
incluyendo control de clientes, vehículos, entradas, tickets y pagos.

Se desarrolló la misma estructura en dos motores de bases de datos:
**MySQL 8.0** y **PostgreSQL 14**, permitiendo comparar su
funcionamiento, sintaxis y características.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# MySQL 8.0

## Paso 1 — Acceso al motor

``` bash
sudo docker start mysql-server
sudo docker exec -it mysql-server mysql -uroot -p12345
```

## Paso 2 — Creación de la base de datos

``` sql
CREATE DATABASE parksmart;
USE parksmart;
```

## Paso 3 — Creación de tablas

``` sql
CREATE TABLE clients ( 
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  doc_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  doc_number VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  full_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  phone VARCHAR(20),
  email VARCHAR(100),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE vehicles (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  client_id INT NOT NULL,
  plate VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  brand VARCHAR(100),
  description VARCHAR(200),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (client_id) REFERENCES clients(id)
);

CREATE TABLE rates (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  amount_hour INT NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE employees (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  full_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  role VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE shifts (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  employee_id INT NOT NULL,
  start_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  end_time TIMESTAMP,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'open',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (employee_id) REFERENCES employees(id)
);

CREATE TABLE entries (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  vehicle_id INT NOT NULL,
  shift_id INT NOT NULL,
  entry_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  exit_time TIMESTAMP,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (vehicle_id) REFERENCES vehicles(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (shift_id) REFERENCES shifts(id)
);

CREATE TABLE tickets (
  id INT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  entry_id INT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (entry_id) REFERENCES entries(id)
);

CREATE TABLE payments (
  id BIGINT AUTO_INCREMENT PRIMARY KEY,
  ticket_id INT NOT NULL,
  rate_id INT NOT NULL,
  method VARCHAR(50) NOT NULL,
  amount BIGINT NOT NULL,
  pay_date DATE NOT NULL,
  reference VARCHAR(100),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'completed',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ON UPDATE CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  FOREIGN KEY (ticket_id) REFERENCES tickets(id),
  FOREIGN KEY (rate_id) REFERENCES rates(id)
);
```

## Paso 4 — Verificación

``` sql
SHOW TABLES;
```

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-22 143446.png)

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-22 153402.png)

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-22 160521.png)

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-22 171834.png)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# PostgreSQL 14

## Paso 1 — Acceso al motor

``` bash
sudo docker start postgres-server
docker exec -it postgres-server psql -Uroot
```

## Paso 2 — Crear base de datos

``` sql
CREATE DATABASE parksmart;
\c parksmart
```

## Paso 3 — Creación de tablas

``` sql
CREATE TABLE clients ( 
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  doc_type VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL,
  doc_number VARCHAR(30) NOT NULL UNIQUE,
  full_name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
  phone VARCHAR(20),
  email VARCHAR(100),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE vehicles (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  client_id INT REFERENCES clients(id),
  plate VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
  brand VARCHAR(100),
  description VARCHAR(200),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE rates (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name VARCHAR(100),
  amount_hour INT,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE employees (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  full_name VARCHAR(100),
  role VARCHAR(50),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE shifts (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  employee_id INT REFERENCES employees(id),
  start_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  end_time TIMESTAMP,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'open',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE entries (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  vehicle_id INT REFERENCES vehicles(id),
  shift_id INT REFERENCES shifts(id),
  entry_time TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  exit_time TIMESTAMP,
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'active',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE tickets (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  entry_id INT UNIQUE REFERENCES entries(id),
  name VARCHAR(100),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);

CREATE TABLE payments (
  id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  ticket_id INT REFERENCES tickets(id),
  rate_id INT REFERENCES rates(id),
  method VARCHAR(50),
  amount BIGINT,
  pay_date DATE,
  reference VARCHAR(100),
  status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'completed',
  created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
);
```

## Paso 4 — Verificación

``` sql
\dt
```

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-24 075813.png)

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-24 080956.png)

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-24 105315.png)

![](images/Captura de pantalla 2026-03-24 210407.png)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Diferencias entre Motores

| Concepto                 | MySQL             | PostgreSQL        |
|--------------------------|-------------------|-------------------|
| Auto incremento          | AUTO_INCREMENT    | SERIAL            |
| Fecha actual             | CURRENT_TIMESTAMP | CURRENT_TIMESTAMP |
| Actualización automática | Sí                | No                |
| Ver tablas               | SHOW TABLES       | \dt               |

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Modelo de Datos

## Tablas

1.  clients
2.  vehicles
3.  rates
4.  employees
5.  shifts
6.  entries
7.  tickets
8.  payments

## Relaciones

-   clients → vehicles
-   vehicles → entries
-   employees → shifts
-   shifts → entries
-   entries → tickets
-   tickets → payments
-   rates → payments

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Conclusión

Se logró implementar correctamente la base de datos ParkSmart en dos
motores diferentes, evidenciando las diferencias en sintaxis y
comportamiento, especialmente en el manejo de claves autoincrementales y
timestamps.

El modelo permite gestionar de manera eficiente la operación de un
parqueadero, manteniendo integridad referencial entre las entidades
principales del sistema.
